mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of RSAFS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) coupled with declining industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a stagflationary environment, where economic growth is weak while prices rise, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3% AND INDPRO 3-month change < -0.5%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently positive during periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3% and INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% over three months.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of strong economic growth, robust housing starts (HOUST) signal continued demand and investment in the economy, providing a positive short-term boost to market sentiment and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If, during periods of confirmed economic expansion, HOUST exceeding 1500k units does not lead to positive S&P 500 5-day returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A significant slowdown in non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth indicates a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below 50,000 persons.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and overall economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with the year-over-year growth of INDPRO over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is low and retail sales (RSAFS) are showing strong growth, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting robust consumer demand, which is a positive short-term signal for equities.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS is below 2.0% and RSAFS shows strong 3-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic uncertainty or slowdown, a significant decline in GDP (GDPC1) signals a broad contraction in economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings decline.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a confirmed quarter-over-quarter contraction in GDPC1 during an economic recession.
